FIRST card in the entire run and chunk everything by it. 7 module cards

fix(plugin): make activate() sync, namespace CLI under `coc`, robust pkg resolve

Three issues surfaced during the first live-fire test in OpenClaw
(post-1.0.2 publish):

1. **activate() must be synchronous.** OpenClaw's plugin host requires
   hooks/tools to register before the activate call returns. Our
   previous async activate() triggered:
     "claw-mem failed during register: plugin register must be synchronous"
   Refactored:
   - Database.openSync() using the static import of node:sqlit

Project" resource to let DSM manage the compose lifecycle directly.

perf: improve tool calls in reasoning and multiple tool calls display (#7742)

* perf: improve tool calls in reasoning and multiple tool calls display

- Updated LiveChatRoute and OpenApiRoute to replace manual message accumulation with BotMessageAccumulator.
- Simplified message saving logic by using build_bot_history_content and collect_plain_text_from_message_parts.

feat: auto-enable mlx_enable_auto_tool_choice when model.supports_tools=True

When loading MLX models, if the model registry indicates supports_tools=True,
and the load request doesn't explicitly override it, automatically enable
the --enable-auto-tool-choice flag for mlx-openai-server.

This ensures tool-capable models like Qwen 3.6 return structured tool_calls
instead of embedding raw tool syntax in content text.

fix: prevent gateway token drift between openclaw.json and gateway-token file (#166)

* fix: prevent gateway token drift between openclaw.json and gateway-token file

When regenerateOpenClawConfig() writes a new config, it now also syncs the
gateway-token file so Pinchy's startup reader and OpenClaw always agree on
the active token. server.ts now reads the token from openclaw.json first
(always current) instead of gateway-token (potentially stale after an

fix(startup): suppress false "Cortex offline" warnings from flaky /health

- Retry the startup /health probe twice before giving up, smoothing over
  transient misses on cold Lambda wake-ups.
- If /health still fails, fall back to the knowledge/stats endpoints
  before declaring the API unreachable — a successful read proves the
  API is reachable even when /health is not.
- Log "Cortex connected" (not "offline") whenever the fallback succeeds,
  and keep the offline message only when real reads

Self-heal missing data dir in postupgrade and start-stop-status

An earlier failed install left DSM with a registered openclaw package
but /volume1/docker/openclaw/ missing. When the user retried via "Update",
postupgrade ran (not postinst) and didn't create the data dir, so
start-stop-status then failed with:

    start-stop-status: line 6: cd: /volume1/docker/openclaw: No such
    file or directory

- postupgrade: mkdir -p the data dirs before anything else. Idempotent;
  noop when they alread

Make install scripts permissive + defensive logging

DSM showed a generic "couldn't install" error which masks which script
failed. Rework scripts so the install always completes (even if pieces
are missing), with clear logging, and rely on start-stop-status to
surface the actual error if docker isn't wired up correctly.

  membership at user-create time. Remove manual 'synogroup --memberadd'

fix(web): composer caret stays aligned when @-tagging (#239)

Mirror-overlay chips rendered with 4–5px horizontal padding and
`font-size: 0.9em`, taking more horizontal space than the raw characters
in the textarea behind them. The textarea's caret is positioned by char
count; the chip is positioned by rendered width. After the first chip
the two diverged by a few pixels and the visible caret lagged further
behind with every subsequent chip or typed character.

feat: layered security hardening for untrusted inbound mail

Every inbound message is now treated as untrusted input and runs
through a layered gate before the body reaches the OpenClaw agent.

Identity & reply target
- Envelope-From (Return-Path, stamped by the receiving MTA) is the
  identity of record for routing and the reply target. Header-From
  is retained for transparency but never trusted. Reply `to` in the
  SMTP deliver closure is hardcoded to envelope-from — agent output
  cannot red
Merge branch 'main' of github.com:GaoZimeng0425/NemoNotch

fix(container): scope orphan reaper by install label so peers don't kill each other

Two installs on the same host could trash each other's containers: the
reaper used `docker ps --filter name=nanoclaw-`, a substring match that
picked up every install's containers. A crash-looping peer (e.g. a legacy
v1 plist respawning ~6k times) would call cleanupOrphans on every boot and
kill the healthy install's session containers within seconds of spawn.

- Stamp `--label nanoclaw-install=<slug>` onto ever

fix: limit builtin web search usage (#14466)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:

Built-in web search could be called repeatedly during a single assistant
response, producing multiple large search/citation payloads and very
high token usage.

After this PR:

Built-in web search is bounded for a single response: prepared queries
are deduplicated and capped, repeated tool executions reuse cached
results, later tool-loop steps cannot call the built-in web search tool
again, and returned source

feat: add /crawl pagination command for multi-page structured extraction

/crawl <css-selector> <fields> [max-pages] repeatedly clicks the
next-page button (matched by CSS selector), runs Gemini extraction on
each page, and accumulates a JSON array of all results.  Stops when the

green Syntax

fix(proxy): reset subprocess timeout on each stream chunk

The subprocess timeout was a fixed lifetime cap: 300s after spawn the
proxy SIGTERM'd `claude`, regardless of whether tokens were streaming.
Complex Opus prompts (10-20min) hit this wall every time, openclaw
retried 4x with compaction, then failed over to Gemini.

Convert to an idle timer: reset on every stdout/stderr chunk. Long
prompts work as long as output keeps flowing; only genuinely stuck
subprocesses get killed. Override via OPEN

fix(e2e): bump afterAll hook timeout for pagination tests

PR2's pagination tests create ~112 entries (27 + 85 relation targets
plus parent databases). Archiving them on teardown at Notion's 3 req/sec
ceiling exceeds the 60s default, tripping a hook timeout that marked
the whole suite as failed even when tests passed. Bump to 300s so
teardown has room for real-world retry variance.

build: cache the pnpm store across builds and add a one-time cache-warm helper

`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` runs on every `manage.sh rebuild` and on
Jetson Orin over home broadband takes ~12 min because every tarball is
re-fetched from the npm registry into a fresh per-build store. For a
project with ~300 workspace packages that's the single largest slice of
the ~32 min build budget.

Two changes:

1. Re-enable the BuildKit cache mount on the pnpm store (Dockerfile):

     RUN --mount=type=
构建参数移到 build.conf，由文件决定而非写死

media: resolve absolute filesystem paths (leading /) for --upload

When callers rewrite '../assets/foo.png' to '/private/tmp/assets/foo.png'
before conversion (a common preprocessing step for design exports),

file's directory and fail. Now an absolute path starting with / is
treated as a filesystem location directly when the file exists.

Date.now() on every inbound message. This prevented session idle
and daily reset from ever firing, since evaluateSessionFreshness()
always saw a fresh updatedAt.

The fix from #32379 patched recordSessionMetaFromInbound to use
mergeSessionEntryPreserveActivity, but missed updateLastRoute() in
the same inbound pipeline.

fix(setup): add npm global prefix bin to PATH after fallback install

When corepack enable fails with EACCES (common when Node is installed to a system-writable prefix like /usr/local that the user doesn't own), we fall back to `npm install -g pnpm`. But npm's global prefix isn't always on the shell's PATH — users often set `npm config set prefix ~/.npm-global` to avoid sudo, and the resulting bin dir isn't picked up by `command -v`. in Coolify's container is read-only or owned by a different uid — so
`npx playwright install chromium` ran but the binary never landed,
and the launch immediately re-failed with "Executable doesn't exist".

Force PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/tmp/ms-playwright at module load (so
chromium.executablePath() resolves to /tmp) and pass the same env var
through to th

color from inside the parens and use it as a solid approximation.

fix(sync): harden skill discovery

Follow symlinks during skill discovery by using Path::is_dir instead of
DirEntry::file_type which returns the symlink type rather than the
target. Add a depth limit (32) to copy_dir_contents to prevent stack
overflow from symlink cycles. Warn when a subdirectory skill shadows
a root-level SKILL.md. Clean up redundant variable rebinding in
copy_file.

Switch updater to track Baileys npm releases (self-hosted code)

Limor is installed per-user (self-hosted), so checking GitHub tags for
releases doesn't apply anymore. The bot now polls npm for new Baileys
versions and notifies the owner; the user runs `npm run update:baileys`
when they want to upgrade the WhatsApp library.

  `--baileys` flag upgrades @whiskeysockets/baileys specifically

(e.g. system=3.12, uv tool=3.11), cffi's C extension version mismatches.

any imports — transparent to the agent, no wrapper script needed.

generated HTML has a single /open-harness/ prefix.

in the billing header and user-agent string, causing a version mismatch between
the proxy fingerprint and the actual installed binary.

- CC_VERSION: 2.1.114 → 2.1.117
- Proxy VERSION: 2.4.2 → 2.4.3

site still said v1.0.2 until I noticed.

Now on page load index.html calls

feat(watchers): add llm-failure-watcher for BYOK-key fallback replies

Tails openclaw.service journal for auth / billing / rate-limit errors
from the customer's own LLM key. On a match, HMAC-signs a POST to the
portal's /api/droplet/fallback/generate endpoint which runs a short
Gemini 2.5 Flash reply on Bintra's funded key (capped at $5/month
globally in the portal) and sends the reply text to Telegram so
Manager never goes fully mute when a BYOK key fails.

## Summary

This adds the first version of a native harness memory bridge. Codex native memory remains in Codex, while Signet indexes the relevant files as external memory artifacts with harness provenance so they can be recalled through Signet across connectors.

The implementation adds a generic native memory source layer in the daemon, Codex file discovery under `~/.codex/memories`, artifact indexing without materializing duplicate `memories

feat(kernel): add wakeAgent gate for cron script pre-check (#3010)

* feat(kernel): add wakeAgent gate for cron script output

AgentTurn cron jobs now support an optional pre_check_script field.
Before waking the agent, the script runs; if its last non-empty stdout
line is {"wakeAgent": false} (strict bool), the agent call is skipped
and the job is recorded as a success — no LLM spend. Any other output,
non-JSON, or missing key defaults to waking normally.

* fix(types): add pre_check_script fie

fix: audio duration from ffprobe manifest + humanizer cleanup

Two fixes heard/seen in production:

1. Duration stuck at 0:00 — Edge TTS MP3s lack Xing/Info headers, so
   browsers can't read total duration from metadata alone. Fixed by
   pre-computing durations with ffprobe locally and committing them
   as src/data/audio-manifest.json. ListenToPost imports this at
   build time (works on Vercel — no ffprobe needed server-side).
   The player now shows 6:05 / 5:11 instead of 5:11 / 0:00.

fix(plugin): prevent session expiry during poll-only cycles (v0.5.6)

Three fixes to ensure sessions stay alive when the plugin is polling
but no messages are being sent:

1. service-loop: renew sessions with unknown expiresAt (null) on
   first tick, with sentinel to prevent infinite retry on dead sessions

2. runtime: recover expiresAt via status() when join returns
   agent_id_taken (plugin restart while already in session)

3. cli: send empty JSON body on renew without --ttl to prevent
   se

DMG/EXE don't need to change. Save ~25 min of Tauri compile per patch
release by reusing the previous tag's binaries with a filename rename.

Workflow now has four jobs:

  plan    → compare this tag's MAJOR.MINOR to previous tag's; sets
            rebuild=true/false and exposes prev_tag
  test    → always runs (shellcheck, bash tests, cargo test, docker

feat(webhook): add deliver_only mode for zero-LLM push notifications (#3011)

Incoming webhook POSTs on a deliver_only route are forwarded directly to
the configured delivery channel (e.g. telegram) without invoking any

the deliver field or set it to "log".

feat(slack): add reactions_enabled toggle for processing state indicators (#3009)

Add SLACK_REACTIONS env var (default true) to control whether the Slack
adapter adds/removes reaction emojis during message processing. When enabled,
adds "eyes" on receipt and swaps to "white_check_mark" after reply. All
reaction API calls are fail-open (warn only, never block message flow).

feat(signal): add media attachment delivery support (#3008)

Handle Image, File, FileData, Voice, Video, Audio, and Animation
content types by downloading the URL and sending as base64-encoded

adapter's built shell command through subprocess.run(shell=True),
which fed a POSIX single-quoted python3 -c string into Windows'
cmd.exe and made it choke on "unterminated string literal".

via sys.executable, bypassing the host shell entirely. Also skip the
class on Windows — the adapter onl

fix(wasm): remove stale 10M fuel limit from settings DB (#2851)

* fix(wasm): remove stale 10M fuel limit from settings DB

Databases that persisted `wasm.default_fuel_limit = 10000000` before
the code default was bumped to 500M (limits.rs, config/wasm.rs) still
read the old value at startup because DB settings take priority over
code defaults. This caused WASM tools like google_slides to fail with
"Fuel exhausted: execution exceeded 10000000 fuel units" even though
the code default is 500M.

Codex peer-review caught this before it shipped. The rules module doc
and the ClawguardPolicyEngine type claimed "session-local" rate
limiting, but the engine held a single shared RateLimiter and fed it to
every payload regardless of session_id. An adversary rotating session
IDs through a single broker process would dodge the cap completely —
the same class of bypass we already guard against in destructive-token

files in /tmp/openclaw/. Updated readGatewayLogs() to:

vercel.json currently allow-lists SVG-only hosts (img.shields.io,
shields.io, badgen.net, flat.badgen.net) while dangerouslyAllowSVG:
false rejects every SVG source. Those two settings are incompatible,
and every badge in every README on production is returning 400
INVALID_IMAGE_OPTIMIZE_REQUEST (e.g. the license badge on

vercel: rm symlinks before cp so Vercel build succeeds

Previous buildCommand did 'cp -f scripts/install.sh website/' but
website/install.sh is a symlink to that same file — cp refuses to
copy a file onto itself, build fails with exit 1. Prepend rm -f so
the symlinks are removed first, then real file contents get placed.
Local dev symlinks stay as-is; they're only ever replaced inside
Vercel's build sandbox.

  can

``InlineKeyboardButton(label, callback_data=label)`` fails Telegram's
API when the label exceeds 64 bytes UTF-8. An LLM-generated long
option (realistic in multilingual flows) used to 400 the ``send_message``
call silently — user got nothing, agent heard a successful retry-then-drop.

fix(guard): skip duplicate pipeline run on Nio self-invocation

When a skill or E2E flow runs Nio's action-cli via Bash on Claude Code,
the outer PreToolUse guard hook was analysing the Bash command string
via Phase 1-6 and then action-cli was analysing the same envelope
again in its subprocess. The outer pass frequently denied the skill
query outright when the skill arg carried literal dangerous tokens,
so Nio's own skill could not be used to ask about dangerous commands.

feat(#112): Caddy gateway — oh expose <name> <port> with laptop/cloud parity

Adds a Caddy reverse-proxy sidecar and a new primary exposure path:
`oh expose <name> <port>` routes a sandbox app via docker-network DNS.
The URL shape is a function of host mode (laptop vs remote), not a flag.

Laptop (default): https://<name>.<sandbox>.localhost:8443 with tls internal.
Remote (PUBLIC_DOMAIN set): https://<name>.<sandbox>.<PUBLIC_DOMAIN>
  via ACME or a Cloudflare named tunnel (future overlay).

to setup-node.sh instead.

fix(telegram): fall back buttons to inline text when keyboard disabled

Buttons are semantic options, not a separate channel protocol: a user
who taps "Yes" and a user who types "yes" arrive at the agent as the
same string. Dropping ``msg.buttons`` when ``inline_keyboards=False``
was the worst of both worlds — the agent got told "Message sent with
N button(s)" while the user saw a question with no options.

Splice the labels into the message text instead. The LLM produces the
same ``message(butt

fix(smart): unbreak OAuth callback on Cloud Run

Two bugs in the same code path made the SMART patient picker appear
to crash. Sandbox returned a valid OAuth code; our callback then died.

1. Prisma client only shipped a query engine for the build host's
   OpenSSL. Cloud Run runs Debian Bookworm (3.0.x), so the first
   db.user.upsert() in the callback threw "could not locate the
   Query Engine for runtime debian-openssl-3.0.x". Add the target
   to schema.prisma so the build emits both engine

test(gpu_guard): fix test_half_open_failure_reopens_circuit

Test was using cooldown_seconds=0.0 and asserting is_available()
returns False after the probe failure re-opened the circuit. But
cooldown=0.0 means ANY subsequent is_available() call transitions
OPEN -> HALF_OPEN immediately (elapsed >= 0), so the assertion was
mechanically impossible under the test's own setup.

Fix: use cooldown_seconds=0.1, sleep past the initial window to
deliberately trigger the HALF_OPEN transition, record a pro

   bypasses the gate.

fix(db): v2_versioning trigger chokes on backslash content (v3.1)

The mnemos_version_snapshot() trigger computes content-addressed
commit_hashes via:

    sha256((id || '|' || v || '|' || content || '|' || now)::bytea)

Direct text::bytea cast interprets backslash-escape-like sequences
(\x47, \0, \r, \d+) as bytea escape syntax. Postgres rejects
memories whose content contains those sequences with:

    invalid input syntax for type bytea

That's extremely common content — Windows paths, shell

fix(device-vars): refuse legacy empty-wipe on non-empty vault (#1995)

2026-04-23 incident: legacy POST /api/device-vars with {vars:{}} and no
source silently erased 17 live keys. The legacy path ("replace all, no
merge") remains for intentional full resets, but an EMPTY replacement
against a non-empty vault is almost always a bug — a curl with wrong
payload, a script dropping the source field, a retry loop sending its
initial state.

Guard: when incoming vars is empty AND no source field is pre

add n8n_trigger tool (webhook + workflow-id)

Two modes:
- mode='webhook' POSTs (or GET/PUT/DELETE) to a webhook path with an
  optional JSON payload. This is the reliable path.
- mode='workflow' hits POST /api/v1/workflows/:id/execute after a
  pre-check that the workflow is active and has a trigger node
  matching the webhook/manual/form allowlist. Most n8n builds don't
  expose this endpoint; on 404/405 the tool surfaces a hint to
  switch to webhook mode.

Replaces fragile manual ENV save/restore blocks with a shared Monitor-synchronized
with_env helper, and switches Rails test parallelization from threads to processes.

Why:
- Thread-based parallelism shares ENV memory between workers, causing
  cross-test pollution when tests mutate ENV directly.
- Manual previous=ENV[key]; ENV[key]=x; ensure ENV[key]=previous is verbose
  and easy to get wrong (missed res

fix: handle symlinks during backup copy (#14471)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:

Windows backups could fail for non-admin users when the Data directory
contained symlinks, because the backup copy attempted to recreate
symlink entries and hit `EPERM: operation not permitted, symlink`.

After this PR:

Backup copy dereferences valid symlinks and stores the target file or
directory contents in the backup. Broken or unreadable symlinks are
skipped with a warning. Restore copy skips symlink

## Bug: `openclaw` missing from PATH for non-interactive SSH (approve-pairing)

Default Ubuntu `.bashrc` starts with a non-interactive early-return. Stock
`ssh host 'openclaw ...'` never sources nvm, so `openclaw` (which lives under
`~/.nvm/versions/node/v<ver>/bin/`) is not on PATH. Every `approve-pairing`
call would fail with `openclaw: command not found` on a clean install.

Fix: prepend `export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm";

feat(memory): vacuum sqlite after session prune at startup (#3002)

Add `MemorySubstrate::vacuum_if_shrank(pruned_count)` that runs
`PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` followed by `VACUUM` only when
rows were actually deleted (mirrors Hermes `maybe_auto_prune_and_vacuum`).

Call it once at kernel boot in `start_background_agents`, after a
one-shot session prune (expired + excess), so the DB file is
compacted on startup without the overhead of an unconditional VACUUM.

not update once initialized. A page kept open across midnight — or one where

a historical window, and today's activity is filtered out by
`src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts`'s per-entry timesta

fix(security): recognize Docker ULA IPv6 addresses as private (#1686)

* fix(security): recognize Docker ULA IPv6 addresses as private

host.docker.internal resolves to fdc4:f303:9324::254 inside Docker
Desktop containers. The IPv6 private-range check used startsWith('fd00::')
which missed ULA addresses outside the fd00:: literal prefix. Match on
the two-character hex prefix ('fc'/'fd') instead, covering the full
fc00::/7 ULA range per RFC 4193.

Fixes #1685

rather than launching a local Chromium child process.

Endpoint formats accepted:

- `ws://host:port/devtools/browser/<id>` — opens

operators fine-grained control over how long individual tools may run

Resolution order for tool `T`:
1. Exact key match in `[tool_timeouts]`
2. First glob match in `[tool_timeouts]` (via `glob_matches`)

The bot-auth filter used LIKE '%->${id}%', which only matched the first
recipient of a multi-target broadcast source like `entity:3:LOBSTER->1,2,4,5`.

after a comma, not after the `->` arrow.

`->` or `,` on the left and `,` or end-of-string on the right, so every
rec

feat: configurable memoryMode (sync default, async opt-in)

Adds a `memoryMode` plugin config option (and `SONZAI_MEMORY_MODE` env
override) that controls the Sonzai Mind Layer's supplementary memory
recall timing. Defaults to "sync" — the plugin blocks context build
until recall returns, so every fact lands in the current turn.
Per-turn completeness matters more than first-token latency for
OpenClaw's typical usage.

Users who have measured first-token latency as a blocker can opt into
"async"

fix(jetson): use python3 to patch daemon.json instead of sed (#1913)

## Problem

The `setup-jetson.sh` script used `sed` to patch
`/etc/docker/daemon.json` on JP6 devices. The sed command stripped the
trailing comma from `"default-runtime": "nvidia",`:

```
s/"default-runtime": "nvidia",/"default-runtime": "nvidia"/
```

This produced invalid JSON when `"runtimes"` was the next key:
```json
{
    "default-runtime": "nvidia"   ← missing comma here
    "runtimes": {
        "nvidia": { ... }

kind: "model", retry: "model" }`, so the expired-key case never reaches
the credential-message regex. In the Gemini validation flow (no
`allowModelRetry`), `kind: "model"` falls through to `unknown`, and the

fix: disable mixed precision stats to prevent numpy OOM on 3D MoE tensors

NNCF mixed precision hessian stats compute x*x on activation tensors.
For MoE expert layers with shape [128, seq_len, 2048], this creates
1+ GB numpy arrays causing _ArrayMemoryError. With ratio=1.0 (all-INT4),
mixed precision sensitivity stats are unnecessary. Pass
enable_mixed_precision=False to register_all_statistics().

the app with the generic "Could not create or load device identity" error.
Wire expo-crypto (already installed) as the SHA-512/SHA-256 provider and
drop the dead webcrypto backend from device-identity.

0.1.7: force openclaw agent --local (skip gateway handshake)

The in-container gateway isn't present, so openclaw's default path
attempts a gateway handshake, fails with a 1006 close, and emits a
noisy "Gateway agent failed; falling back to embedded" warning before
dropping into embedded mode. Passing --local makes OpenClaw skip the
gateway attempt entirely and go straight to embedded, which is the
only mode that ever works inside a Harbor task container.

README: update message format docs to reflect JSON envelope

Pass full SQS message as JSON to subagents

Subagents receive the complete SQS Message object (Body, MessageId,
ReceiptHandle, Attributes, MessageAttributes, etc.) serialized as
JSON. This preserves forward compatibility — future subagents can
extract whatever fields they need, and we can add message attribute
support without a breaking change.

The dry-run log was also updated to show the full message.

fix(sdk,tasks): retry 429s in-loop with backoff; cool down after task failure

sdk.rs: when the API returns 429, sleep up to 120s (honouring Retry-After
if present) and retry the same turn rather than propagating it as a fatal
error. Prevents tasks from being unclaimed and immediately re-hammered
across the fleet when the inference endpoint is saturated.

The folder-focus useEffect listed contentBounds in its deps. Whenever
the user zoomed across a bucket threshold (e.g. day → 6-hour), the
bucketStep state updated, timelineBands recomputed, contentBounds got
a new object reference, and the useEffect re-fired — calling fitTo
which slammed the camera back to fit-out. Every attempt to zoom in
got reset, and the smooth day→hour transition never had a chance to
play.

fix(setup): fall back to npm install when corepack is missing

Some Node installs (older nvm, node@22 keg-only on brew, minimal distro packages) don't ship corepack, so the bootstrap was dying with "corepack: command not found" before pnpm could land on PATH. Now guards the corepack call and falls back to `npm install -g pnpm@<pinned>`, reading the version from package.json's packageManager field.

fix(docs-i18n): route params match content/docs/<lang>/<slug>.mdx tree

Fumadocs 16.8 loader.getPages() returns the flat collection, not
per-locale filtered, even with i18n enabled. Our content lives at
content/docs/<lang>/<slug>.mdx — so every page's slugs[0] IS the
locale. Split it at generateStaticParams, and mirror in the
getPage() lookup with [lang, ...slug] composed key. Result: routes
now emit correctly as /<lang>/docs/<slug> (was /<lang>/docs/<lang>/<slug>).

fix(word): bookmark/comment/pagebreak/sdt now honor --index/--after/--before

Same pattern as #76: these AddXxx helpers took an 'index' parameter but
ignored it, silently appending to the end of the paragraph.

- AddBreak: insert break run at child-element index
- AddBookmark: positional insert when anchor is supplied, bypassing the
  text-match wrap path (anchor wins over property-based search)
- AddComment: insert rangeStart/rangeEnd/refRun triple at child-element
  index for paragraph-parent

feat(mindmap): Route A mirror — auto-pair notes with mindmap leaves (#1984)

Every mission note gets a matching mindmap leaf under a lazily-created
domain node for its category. Note anchors (`note`) and category anchors
(`note_category`, keyed on lowercased/trimmed category) make lookups O(1)
and idempotent, so create/update/delete are safe to retry and the backfill
can be rerun without duplicating nodes.

Integration is fire-and-forget from mission.js: the note write is the
source of truth, an

Addresses codex review P2 on install.sh:103-111.

When `openclaw` is on PATH but `agents add --workspace` fails (older forks
that don't support the subcommand or flag), the previous failure message
told the user to retry the same unsupported command. That left users on
older OpenClaw builds without a working setup even though install.sh had
already written `.openclaw-system.md` — the exact backward-compat include
those bu

0.1.2: parse sessions/*.jsonl as a usage fallback

The structured `openclaw.events.jsonl` stream only gets written when
OpenClaw runs against a gateway. In the common case today the in-
container gateway fails the loopback handshake and OpenClaw silently
falls back to embedded mode, which does NOT emit events.jsonl — the
only place usage lands is `message.usage` inside each session
transcript message (camelCase fields: `input` / `output` / `cacheRead`
/ `cacheWrite` / `cost.total`).
